text.title: AI-Assisted Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-assisted-accessibility methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Report MUST combine automated scan + human-AT test results; either alone is incomplete.
rule.rationale: Public WCAG guidance: automation insufficient for conformance claim.

rule.statement: Human-AT testing MUST cover the criteria automation cannot evaluate (cognitive / screen reader semantics / voice navigation).
rule.rationale: Without the coverage report is non-conformant by definition.

rule.statement: Output MUST be a signed JSON or PDF, not a dashboard link.
Report MUST schedule re-audit ≤12 months later.
rule.rationale: WCAG is a moving target; annual cadence is conformance norm.

rule.statement: Every reported issue MUST cite scan log id OR tester id + transcript.
rule.rationale: Without traceable evidence, conformance claims fall apart in audit.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
